43	func buildToolUsageInfo(metrics LogMetrics) []ToolUsageInfo {
44		toolStats := make(map[string]*ToolUsageInfo)
45	
46		for _, toolCall := range metrics.ToolCalls {
47			displayKey := workflow.PrettifyToolName(toolCall.Name)
48			if existing, exists := toolStats[displayKey]; exists {
49				existing.CallCount += toolCall.CallCount
50				if toolCall.MaxInputSize > existing.MaxInputSize {
51					existing.MaxInputSize = toolCall.MaxInputSize
52				}
53				if toolCall.MaxOutputSize > existing.MaxOutputSize {
54					existing.MaxOutputSize = toolCall.MaxOutputSize
55				}
56				if toolCall.MaxDuration > 0 {
57					maxDuration := timeutil.FormatDuration(toolCall.MaxDuration)
58					if existing.MaxDuration == "" || toolCall.MaxDuration > parseDurationString(existing.MaxDuration) {
59						existing.MaxDuration = maxDuration
60					}
61				}
62				continue
63			}
64	
65			info := &ToolUsageInfo{
66				Name:          displayKey,
67				CallCount:     toolCall.CallCount,
68				MaxInputSize:  toolCall.MaxInputSize,
69				MaxOutputSize: toolCall.MaxOutputSize,
70			}
71			if toolCall.MaxDuration > 0 {
72				info.MaxDuration = timeutil.FormatDuration(toolCall.MaxDuration)
73			}
74			toolStats[displayKey] = info
75		}
76	
77		toolUsage := make([]ToolUsageInfo, 0, len(toolStats))
78		for _, info := range toolStats {
79			toolUsage = append(toolUsage, *info)
80		}
81	
82		slices.SortFunc(toolUsage, func(a, b ToolUsageInfo) int {
83			if a.CallCount != b.CallCount {
84				return b.CallCount - a.CallCount
85			}
86			return strings.Compare(a.Name, b.Name)
87		})
88	
89		return toolUsage
90	}

92	func deriveRunAgenticAnalysis(processedRun ProcessedRun, metrics LogMetrics) (*AwContext, []ToolUsageInfo, []CreatedItemReport, *TaskDomainInfo, *BehaviorFingerprint, []AgenticAssessment) {
93		auditAgenticLog.Printf("Deriving agentic analysis for run: id=%d workflow=%s", processedRun.Run.DatabaseID, processedRun.Run.WorkflowName)
94		var awContext *AwContext
95		if processedRun.AwContext != nil {
96			awContext = processedRun.AwContext
97		} else if processedRun.Run.LogsPath != "" {
98			awInfoPath := filepath.Join(processedRun.Run.LogsPath, "aw_info.json")
99			if info, err := parseAwInfo(awInfoPath, false); err == nil && info != nil {
100				awContext = info.Context
101			}
102		}
103	
104		toolUsage := buildToolUsageInfo(metrics)
105		createdItems := extractCreatedItemsFromManifest(processedRun.Run.LogsPath)
106		metricsData := MetricsData{
107			TokenUsage:    processedRun.Run.TokenUsage,
108			EstimatedCost: processedRun.Run.EstimatedCost,
109			ActionMinutes: processedRun.Run.ActionMinutes,
110			Turns:         processedRun.Run.Turns,
111			ErrorCount:    processedRun.Run.ErrorCount,
112			WarningCount:  processedRun.Run.WarningCount,
113		}
114	
115		taskDomain := detectTaskDomain(processedRun, createdItems, toolUsage, awContext)
116		behaviorFingerprint := buildBehaviorFingerprint(processedRun, metricsData, toolUsage, createdItems, awContext)
117		agenticAssessments := buildAgenticAssessments(processedRun, metricsData, toolUsage, createdItems, taskDomain, behaviorFingerprint, awContext)
118	
119		auditAgenticLog.Printf("Agentic analysis complete: tool_types=%d created_items=%d assessments=%d", len(toolUsage), len(createdItems), len(agenticAssessments))
120		return awContext, toolUsage, createdItems, taskDomain, behaviorFingerprint, agenticAssessments

123	func detectTaskDomain(processedRun ProcessedRun, createdItems []CreatedItemReport, toolUsage []ToolUsageInfo, awContext *AwContext) *TaskDomainInfo {
124		auditAgenticLog.Printf("Detecting task domain for run: workflow=%s event=%s", processedRun.Run.WorkflowName, processedRun.Run.Event)
125		combined := strings.ToLower(strings.Join([]string{
126			processedRun.Run.WorkflowName,
127			processedRun.Run.WorkflowPath,
128			processedRun.Run.Event,
129		}, " "))
130	
131		createdTypes := make([]string, 0, len(createdItems))
132		for _, item := range createdItems {
133			createdTypes = append(createdTypes, strings.ToLower(item.Type))
134		}
135		createdJoined := strings.Join(createdTypes, " ")
136	
137		toolNames := make([]string, 0, len(toolUsage))
138		for _, tool := range toolUsage {
139			toolNames = append(toolNames, strings.ToLower(tool.Name))
140		}
141		toolJoined := strings.Join(toolNames, " ")
142	
143		switch {
144		case containsAny(combined, "release", "deploy", "publish", "backport", "changelog"):
145			return &TaskDomainInfo{Name: "release_ops", Label: "Release / Ops", Reason: "Workflow metadata matches release or operational automation."}
146		case containsAny(combined, "research", "investigat", "analysis", "analy", "report", "audit"):
147			return &TaskDomainInfo{Name: "research", Label: "Research", Reason: "Workflow naming and instructions suggest exploratory analysis or reporting."}
148		case containsAny(combined, "triage", "label", "classif", "route") || containsAny(createdJoined, "add_labels", "remove_labels", "set_issue_type"):
149			return &TaskDomainInfo{Name: "triage", Label: "Triage", Reason: "The run focused on classification, routing, or issue state updates."}
150		case containsAny(combined, "fix", "patch", "repair", "refactor", "swe", "code", "review") || containsAny(createdJoined, "create_pull_request_review_comment", "submit_pull_request_review"):
151			return &TaskDomainInfo{Name: "code_fix", Label: "Code Fix", Reason: "The workflow appears oriented toward code changes or pull request review."}
152		case containsAny(combined, "cleanup", "maint", "update", "deps", "sync", "housekeeping"):
153			return &TaskDomainInfo{Name: "repo_maintenance", Label: "Repo Maintenance", Reason: "Workflow metadata matches repository maintenance or update work."}
154		case containsAny(combined, "issue", "discussion", "comment", "support", "reply") || containsAny(createdJoined, "add_comment", "create_discussion"):
155			return &TaskDomainInfo{Name: "issue_response", Label: "Issue Response", Reason: "The run is primarily interacting with issue, discussion, or comment threads."}
156		case awContext != nil:
157			return &TaskDomainInfo{Name: "delegated_automation", Label: "Delegated Automation", Reason: "The run was dispatched from an upstream workflow and is acting as a delegated task."}
158		case containsAny(toolJoined, "github_issue_read", "github-discussion-query"):
159			return &TaskDomainInfo{Name: "issue_response", Label: "Issue Response", Reason: "Tool usage centers on repository conversations and issue context."}
160		default:
161			return &TaskDomainInfo{Name: "general_automation", Label: "General Automation", Reason: "The run does not strongly match a narrower workflow domain yet."}
162		}
163	}

165	func buildBehaviorFingerprint(processedRun ProcessedRun, metrics MetricsData, toolUsage []ToolUsageInfo, createdItems []CreatedItemReport, awContext *AwContext) *BehaviorFingerprint {
166		auditAgenticLog.Printf("Building behavior fingerprint: run_id=%d turns=%d tool_types=%d created_items=%d", processedRun.Run.DatabaseID, metrics.Turns, len(toolUsage), len(createdItems))
167		toolTypes := len(toolUsage)
168		writeCount := len(createdItems) + processedRun.Run.SafeItemsCount
169	
170		executionStyle := "directed"
171		switch {
172		case metrics.Turns >= 10 || toolTypes >= 6:
173			executionStyle = "exploratory"
174		case metrics.Turns >= 5 || toolTypes >= 4:
175			executionStyle = "adaptive"
176		}
177	
178		toolBreadth := "narrow"
179		switch {
180		case toolTypes >= 6:
181			toolBreadth = "broad"
182		case toolTypes >= 3:
183			toolBreadth = "moderate"
184		}
185	
186		actuationStyle := "read_only"
187		switch {
188		case writeCount >= 6:
189			actuationStyle = "write_heavy"
190		case writeCount > 0:
191			actuationStyle = "selective_write"
192		}
193	
194		resourceProfile := "lean"
195		switch {
196		case processedRun.Run.Duration >= 15*time.Minute || metrics.Turns >= 12 || toolTypes >= 6 || writeCount >= 8:
197			resourceProfile = "heavy"
198		case processedRun.Run.Duration >= 5*time.Minute || metrics.Turns >= 6 || toolTypes >= 4 || writeCount >= 3:
199			resourceProfile = "moderate"
200		}
201	
202		dispatchMode := "standalone"
203		if awContext != nil {
204			dispatchMode = "delegated"
205		}
206	
207		agenticFraction := computeAgenticFraction(processedRun)
208	
209		auditAgenticLog.Printf("Behavior fingerprint: execution=%s breadth=%s actuation=%s resource=%s dispatch=%s agentic_fraction=%.2f", executionStyle, toolBreadth, actuationStyle, resourceProfile, dispatchMode, agenticFraction)
210		return &BehaviorFingerprint{
211			ExecutionStyle:  executionStyle,
212			ToolBreadth:     toolBreadth,
213			ActuationStyle:  actuationStyle,
214			ResourceProfile: resourceProfile,
215			DispatchMode:    dispatchMode,
216			AgenticFraction: agenticFraction,
217		}
218	}
219	
220	func buildAgenticAssessments(processedRun ProcessedRun, metrics MetricsData, toolUsage []ToolUsageInfo, createdItems []CreatedItemReport, domain *TaskDomainInfo, fingerprint *BehaviorFingerprint, awContext *AwContext) []AgenticAssessment {
221		if domain == nil || fingerprint == nil {
222			auditAgenticLog.Print("Skipping agentic assessments: missing domain or fingerprint")
223			return nil
224		}
225		auditAgenticLog.Printf("Building agentic assessments: run_id=%d domain=%s resource=%s execution=%s", processedRun.Run.DatabaseID, domain.Name, fingerprint.ResourceProfile, fingerprint.ExecutionStyle)
226	
227		assessments := make([]AgenticAssessment, 0, 4)
228		toolTypes := len(toolUsage)
229		frictionEvents := len(processedRun.MissingTools) + len(processedRun.MCPFailures) + len(processedRun.MissingData)
230		writeCount := len(createdItems) + processedRun.Run.SafeItemsCount
231	
232		if fingerprint.ResourceProfile == "heavy" {
233			severity := "medium"
234			if metrics.Turns >= 14 || toolTypes >= 7 || processedRun.Run.Duration >= 20*time.Minute {
235				severity = "high"
236			}
237			assessments = append(assessments, AgenticAssessment{
238				Kind:           "resource_heavy_for_domain",
239				Severity:       severity,
240				Summary:        fmt.Sprintf("This %s run consumed a heavy execution profile for its task shape.", domain.Label),
241				Evidence:       fmt.Sprintf("turns=%d tool_types=%d duration=%s write_actions=%d", metrics.Turns, toolTypes, formatAssessmentDuration(processedRun.Run.Duration), writeCount),
242				Recommendation: "Compare this run to similar successful runs and trim unnecessary turns, tools, or write actions.",
243			})
244		}
245	
246		if (domain.Name == "triage" || domain.Name == "repo_maintenance" || domain.Name == "issue_response") && fingerprint.ResourceProfile == "lean" && fingerprint.ExecutionStyle == "directed" && fingerprint.ToolBreadth == "narrow" {
247			assessments = append(assessments, AgenticAssessment{
248				Kind:           "overkill_for_agentic",
249				Severity:       "low",
250				Summary:        fmt.Sprintf("This %s run looks stable enough that deterministic automation may be a simpler fit.", domain.Label),
251				Evidence:       fmt.Sprintf("turns=%d tool_types=%d actuation=%s", metrics.Turns, toolTypes, fingerprint.ActuationStyle),
252				Recommendation: "Consider whether a scripted rule or deterministic workflow step could replace this agentic path.",
253			})
254		}
255	
256		if frictionEvents >= 3 || (frictionEvents > 0 && writeCount >= 3) || ((domain.Name == "triage" || domain.Name == "repo_maintenance" || domain.Name == "issue_response") && fingerprint.ExecutionStyle == "exploratory") {
257			severity := "medium"
258			if frictionEvents >= 4 || (frictionEvents > 0 && fingerprint.ActuationStyle == "write_heavy") {
259				severity = "high"
260			}
261			assessments = append(assessments, AgenticAssessment{
262				Kind:           "poor_agentic_control",
263				Severity:       severity,
264				Summary:        "The run showed signs of broad or weakly controlled agentic behavior.",
265				Evidence:       fmt.Sprintf("friction=%d execution=%s actuation=%s", frictionEvents, fingerprint.ExecutionStyle, fingerprint.ActuationStyle),
266				Recommendation: "Tighten instructions, reduce unnecessary tools, or delay write actions until the workflow has stronger evidence.",
267			})
268		}
269	
270		// Partially reducible: the workflow has a low agentic fraction, meaning
271		// many turns are data-gathering that could be moved to deterministic steps:
272		// or post-steps: in the frontmatter. Only flag when there's substantive work
273		// (not lean/directed runs which overkill_for_agentic already covers).
274		if fingerprint.AgenticFraction > 0 && fingerprint.AgenticFraction < 0.6 &&
275			fingerprint.ResourceProfile != "lean" {
276			severity := "low"
277			if fingerprint.AgenticFraction < 0.4 {
278				severity = "medium"
279			}
280			deterministicPct := int((1.0 - fingerprint.AgenticFraction) * 100)
281			assessments = append(assessments, AgenticAssessment{
282				Kind:           "partially_reducible",
283				Severity:       severity,
284				Summary:        fmt.Sprintf("About %d%% of this run's turns appear to be data-gathering that could move to deterministic steps.", deterministicPct),
285				Evidence:       fmt.Sprintf("agentic_fraction=%.2f turns=%d", fingerprint.AgenticFraction, metrics.Turns),
286				Recommendation: "Move data-fetching work to frontmatter steps: (pre-agent) writing to /tmp/gh-aw/agent/ or post-steps: (post-agent) to reduce inference cost. See the Deterministic & Agentic Patterns guide.",
287			})
288		}
289	
290		// Model downgrade suggestion: the run uses a heavy resource profile but
291		// the task domain is simple enough that a smaller model would likely suffice.
292		if fingerprint.ResourceProfile != "lean" &&
293			(domain.Name == "triage" || domain.Name == "repo_maintenance" || domain.Name == "issue_response") &&
294			fingerprint.ActuationStyle != "write_heavy" {
295			assessments = append(assessments, AgenticAssessment{
296				Kind:           "model_downgrade_available",
297				Severity:       "low",
298				Summary:        fmt.Sprintf("This %s run may not need a frontier model. A smaller model (e.g. gpt-4.1-mini, claude-haiku-4-5) could handle the task at lower cost.", domain.Label),
299				Evidence:       fmt.Sprintf("domain=%s resource_profile=%s actuation=%s", domain.Name, fingerprint.ResourceProfile, fingerprint.ActuationStyle),
300				Recommendation: "Try engine.model: gpt-4.1-mini or claude-haiku-4-5 in the workflow frontmatter.",
301			})
302		}
303	
304		if awContext != nil {
305			assessments = append(assessments, AgenticAssessment{
306				Kind:           "delegated_context_present",
307				Severity:       "info",
308				Summary:        "The run preserved upstream dispatch context, which helps trace multi-workflow episodes.",
309				Evidence:       fmt.Sprintf("workflow_call_id=%s event_type=%s", awContext.WorkflowCallID, awContext.EventType),
310				Recommendation: "Use this context when comparing downstream runs so follow-up workflows are evaluated as part of one task chain.",
311			})
312		}
313	
314		auditAgenticLog.Printf("Built %d agentic assessments", len(assessments))
315		return assessments
316	}
317	
318	func generateAgenticAssessmentFindings(assessments []AgenticAssessment) []Finding {
319		findings := make([]Finding, 0, len(assessments))
320		for _, assessment := range assessments {
321			category := "agentic"
322			impact := "Review recommended"
323			switch assessment.Kind {
324			case "resource_heavy_for_domain":
325				category = "performance"
326				impact = "Higher cost and latency than a comparable well-behaved run"
327			case "overkill_for_agentic":
328				category = "optimization"
329				impact = "A deterministic implementation may be cheaper and easier to govern"
330			case "poor_agentic_control":
331				category = "agentic"
332				impact = "Broad or weakly controlled behavior can reduce trust even when the run succeeds"
333			case "partially_reducible":
334				category = "optimization"
335				impact = "Moving data-gathering turns to deterministic steps reduces inference cost"
336			case "model_downgrade_available":
337				category = "optimization"
338				impact = "A smaller model could reduce per-run cost significantly for this task domain"
339			case "delegated_context_present":
340				category = "coordination"
341				impact = "Context continuity improves downstream debugging and auditability"
342			}
343			findings = append(findings, Finding{
344				Category:    category,
345				Severity:    assessment.Severity,
346				Title:       prettifyAssessmentKind(assessment.Kind),
347				Description: assessment.Summary,
348				Impact:      impact,
349			})
350		}
351		return findings
352	}
353	
354	func generateAgenticAssessmentRecommendations(assessments []AgenticAssessment) []Recommendation {
355		recommendations := make([]Recommendation, 0, len(assessments))
356		for _, assessment := range assessments {
357			if assessment.Recommendation == "" || assessment.Severity == "info" {
358				continue
359			}
360			priority := "medium"
361			if assessment.Severity == "high" {
362				priority = "high"
363			}
364			recommendations = append(recommendations, Recommendation{
365				Priority: priority,
366				Action:   assessment.Recommendation,
367				Reason:   assessment.Summary,
368			})
369		}
370		return recommendations
371	}
372	
373	// computeAgenticFraction classifies tool sequences into reasoning vs. data-gathering
374	// turns and returns the ratio of reasoning turns to total turns.
375	// Reasoning turns are those where the agent makes decisions, writes output, or uses
376	// multiple tool types. Data-gathering turns use only read-oriented tools.
377	func computeAgenticFraction(processedRun ProcessedRun) float64 {
378		run := processedRun.Run
379		if run.Turns <= 0 {
380			auditAgenticLog.Printf("Computing agentic fraction: run_id=%d turns=0, returning 0.0", processedRun.Run.DatabaseID)
381			return 0.0
382		}
383		auditAgenticLog.Printf("Computing agentic fraction: run_id=%d turns=%d safe_items=%d", processedRun.Run.DatabaseID, run.Turns, run.SafeItemsCount)
384	
385		// If no tool sequence data, estimate from write actions
386		writeCount := run.SafeItemsCount
387		if writeCount > 0 && run.Turns > 0 {
388			// At minimum, the fraction of turns that produced write actions are agentic
389			// and non-write turns doing data gathering are deterministic-reducible
390			agenticTurns := writeCount
391			// Add reasoning turns: at least 1 turn of reasoning per write action,
392			// plus initial planning turn
393			reasoningTurns := min(writeCount+1, run.Turns)
394			agenticTurns = max(agenticTurns, reasoningTurns)
395			agenticTurns = min(agenticTurns, run.Turns)
396			return float64(agenticTurns) / float64(run.Turns)
397		}
398	
399		// No write actions: if the run is read-only with few turns, nearly all
400		// turns are reasoning (the AI is analyzing, not just gathering data)
401		if run.Turns <= 3 {
402			return 1.0
403		}
404	
405		// For longer read-only runs, assume early turns gather context and
406		// later turns do analysis. Heuristic: half the turns are reasoning.
407		return 0.5
408	}
409	
410	func containsAny(value string, terms ...string) bool {
411		for _, term := range terms {
412			if strings.Contains(value, term) {
413				return true
414			}
415		}
416		return false
417	}
418	
419	func prettifyAssessmentKind(kind string) string {
420		switch kind {
421		case "resource_heavy_for_domain":
422			return "Resource Heavy For Domain"
423		case "overkill_for_agentic":
424			return "Potential Deterministic Alternative"
425		case "poor_agentic_control":
426			return "Weak Agentic Control"
427		case "delegated_context_present":
428			return "Dispatch Context Preserved"
429		case "partially_reducible":
430			return "Partially Reducible To Deterministic"
431		case "model_downgrade_available":
432			return "Cheaper Model Available"
433		default:
434			return strings.ReplaceAll(kind, "_", " ")
435		}
436	}
437	
438	func formatAssessmentDuration(duration time.Duration) string {
439		if duration <= 0 {
440			return "n/a"
441		}
442		return duration.String()
443	}

238	// buildAuditData creates structured audit data from workflow run information
239	func buildAuditData(processedRun ProcessedRun, metrics LogMetrics, mcpToolUsage *MCPToolUsageData) AuditData {
240		run := processedRun.Run
241		auditReportLog.Printf("Building audit data for run ID %d", run.DatabaseID)
242	
243		// Build overview
244		overview := OverviewData{
245			RunID:        run.DatabaseID,
246			WorkflowName: run.WorkflowName,
247			Status:       run.Status,
248			Conclusion:   run.Conclusion,
249			CreatedAt:    run.CreatedAt,
250			StartedAt:    run.StartedAt,
251			UpdatedAt:    run.UpdatedAt,
252			Event:        run.Event,
253			Branch:       run.HeadBranch,
254			URL:          run.URL,
255		}
256	
257		if run.LogsPath != "" {
258			overview.LogsPath = run.LogsPath
259		}
260	
261		if run.Duration > 0 {
262			overview.Duration = timeutil.FormatDuration(run.Duration)
263		}
264	
265		if run.LogsPath != "" {
266			awInfoPath := filepath.Join(run.LogsPath, "aw_info.json")
267			if awInfo, err := parseAwInfo(awInfoPath, false); err == nil && awInfo != nil {
268				overview.AwContext = awInfo.Context
269			}
270		}
271	
272		// Build metrics
273		metricsData := MetricsData{
274			TokenUsage:    run.TokenUsage,
275			EstimatedCost: run.EstimatedCost,
276			Turns:         run.Turns,
277			ErrorCount:    run.ErrorCount,
278			WarningCount:  run.WarningCount,
279		}
280	
281		// Populate effective tokens from the firewall proxy summary when available,
282		// otherwise fall back to the effective tokens stored on the run itself.
283		if processedRun.TokenUsage != nil && processedRun.TokenUsage.TotalEffectiveTokens > 0 {
284			metricsData.EffectiveTokens = processedRun.TokenUsage.TotalEffectiveTokens
285		} else if run.EffectiveTokens > 0 {
286			metricsData.EffectiveTokens = run.EffectiveTokens
287		}
288	
289		// Populate ActionMinutes from run duration so it is always visible even
290		// when token/turn metrics are zero (e.g. Codex runs that exit early).
291		// Use math.Ceil to match the billable-minute rounding used elsewhere.
292		if run.ActionMinutes > 0 {
293			metricsData.ActionMinutes = run.ActionMinutes
294		} else if run.Duration > 0 {
295			metricsData.ActionMinutes = math.Ceil(run.Duration.Minutes())
296		}
297	
298		// Build job data
299		jobs := sliceutil.Map(processedRun.JobDetails, func(jobDetail JobInfoWithDuration) JobData {
300			job := JobData{
301				Name:       jobDetail.Name,
302				Status:     jobDetail.Status,
303				Conclusion: jobDetail.Conclusion,
304			}
305			if jobDetail.Duration > 0 {
306				job.Duration = timeutil.FormatDuration(jobDetail.Duration)
307			}
308			return job
309		})
310	
311		// Build downloaded files list
312		downloadedFiles := extractDownloadedFiles(run.LogsPath)
313	
314		// For failed workflows where the agent never ran (no agent-stdio.log),
315		// extract errors from step log files to surface the actual failure reason.
316		var errors []ErrorInfo
317		if run.Conclusion == "failure" && run.LogsPath != "" {
318			if stepErrors := extractPreAgentStepErrors(run.LogsPath); len(stepErrors) > 0 {
319				errors = stepErrors
320			}
321		}
322	
323		toolUsage := buildToolUsageInfo(metrics)
324	
325		createdItems := extractCreatedItemsFromManifest(run.LogsPath)
326		taskDomain := detectTaskDomain(processedRun, createdItems, toolUsage, overview.AwContext)
327		behaviorFingerprint := buildBehaviorFingerprint(processedRun, metricsData, toolUsage, createdItems, overview.AwContext)
328		agenticAssessments := buildAgenticAssessments(processedRun, metricsData, toolUsage, createdItems, taskDomain, behaviorFingerprint, overview.AwContext)
329	
330		// Generate key findings
331		findings := generateFindings(processedRun, metricsData, errors)
332		findings = append(findings, generateAgenticAssessmentFindings(agenticAssessments)...)
333	
334		// Generate recommendations
335		recommendations := generateRecommendations(processedRun, metricsData, findings)
336		recommendations = append(recommendations, generateAgenticAssessmentRecommendations(agenticAssessments)...)
337	
338		observabilityInsights := buildAuditObservabilityInsights(processedRun, metricsData, toolUsage, createdItems)
339		observabilityInsights = append(observabilityInsights, buildDrain3Insights(processedRun, metricsData, toolUsage)...)
340	
341		// Generate performance metrics
342		performanceMetrics := generatePerformanceMetrics(processedRun, metricsData, toolUsage)
343	
344		// Extract expanded audit data
345		engineConfig := extractEngineConfig(run.LogsPath)
346		promptAnalysis := extractPromptAnalysis(run.LogsPath)
347		sessionAnalysis := buildSessionAnalysis(processedRun, metrics)
348		safeOutputSummary := buildSafeOutputSummary(createdItems)
349		mcpServerHealth := buildMCPServerHealth(mcpToolUsage, processedRun.MCPFailures)
350	
351		if auditReportLog.Enabled() {
352			auditReportLog.Printf("Built audit data: %d jobs, %d errors, %d tool types, %d findings, %d recommendations",
353				len(jobs), len(errors), len(toolUsage), len(findings), len(recommendations))
354		}
355	
356		return AuditData{
357			Overview:                overview,
358			TaskDomain:              taskDomain,
359			BehaviorFingerprint:     behaviorFingerprint,
360			AgenticAssessments:      agenticAssessments,
361			Metrics:                 metricsData,
362			KeyFindings:             findings,
363			Recommendations:         recommendations,
364			ObservabilityInsights:   observabilityInsights,
365			PerformanceMetrics:      performanceMetrics,
366			EngineConfig:            engineConfig,
367			PromptAnalysis:          promptAnalysis,
368			SessionAnalysis:         sessionAnalysis,
369			SafeOutputSummary:       safeOutputSummary,
370			MCPServerHealth:         mcpServerHealth,
371			Jobs:                    jobs,
372			DownloadedFiles:         downloadedFiles,
373			MissingTools:            processedRun.MissingTools,
374			MissingData:             processedRun.MissingData,
375			Noops:                   processedRun.Noops,
376			MCPFailures:             processedRun.MCPFailures,
377			FirewallTokenUsage:      processedRun.TokenUsage,
378			GitHubRateLimitUsage:    processedRun.GitHubRateLimitUsage,
379			FirewallAnalysis:        processedRun.FirewallAnalysis,
380			PolicyAnalysis:          processedRun.PolicyAnalysis,
381			RedactedDomainsAnalysis: processedRun.RedactedDomainsAnalysis,
382			Errors:                  errors,
383			ToolUsage:               toolUsage,
384			MCPToolUsage:            mcpToolUsage,
385			CreatedItems:            createdItems,
386		}
387	}

package cli

import (
	"bufio"
	"encoding/json"
	"math"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strconv"
	"strings"
	"time"

	"github.com/github/gh-aw/pkg/constants"
	"github.com/github/gh-aw/pkg/logger"
	"github.com/github/gh-aw/pkg/sliceutil"
	"github.com/github/gh-aw/pkg/timeutil"
)

var auditReportLog = logger.New("cli:audit_report")

// AuditData represents the complete structured audit data for a workflow run
type AuditData struct {
	Overview                OverviewData             `json:"overview"`
	Comparison              *AuditComparisonData     `json:"comparison,omitempty"`
	TaskDomain              *TaskDomainInfo          `json:"task_domain,omitempty"`
	BehaviorFingerprint     *BehaviorFingerprint     `json:"behavior_fingerprint,omitempty"`
	AgenticAssessments      []AgenticAssessment      `json:"agentic_assessments,omitempty"`
	Metrics                 MetricsData              `json:"metrics"`
	KeyFindings             []Finding                `json:"key_findings,omitempty"`
	Recommendations         []Recommendation         `json:"recommendations,omitempty"`
	ObservabilityInsights   []ObservabilityInsight   `json:"observability_insights,omitempty"`
	PerformanceMetrics      *PerformanceMetrics      `json:"performance_metrics,omitempty"`
	EngineConfig            *EngineConfig            `json:"engine_config,omitempty"`
	PromptAnalysis          *PromptAnalysis          `json:"prompt_analysis,omitempty"`
	SessionAnalysis         *SessionAnalysis         `json:"session_analysis,omitempty"`
	SafeOutputSummary       *SafeOutputSummary       `json:"safe_output_summary,omitempty"`
	MCPServerHealth         *MCPServerHealth         `json:"mcp_server_health,omitempty"`
	Jobs                    []JobData                `json:"jobs,omitempty"`
	DownloadedFiles         []FileInfo               `json:"downloaded_files"`
	MissingTools            []MissingToolReport      `json:"missing_tools,omitempty"`
	MissingData             []MissingDataReport      `json:"missing_data,omitempty"`
	Noops                   []NoopReport             `json:"noops,omitempty"`
	MCPFailures             []MCPFailureReport       `json:"mcp_failures,omitempty"`
	FirewallTokenUsage      *TokenUsageSummary       `json:"firewall_token_usage,omitempty"`
	GitHubRateLimitUsage    *GitHubRateLimitUsage    `json:"github_rate_limit_usage,omitempty"`
	FirewallAnalysis        *FirewallAnalysis        `json:"firewall_analysis,omitempty"`
	PolicyAnalysis          *PolicyAnalysis          `json:"policy_analysis,omitempty"`
	RedactedDomainsAnalysis *RedactedDomainsAnalysis `json:"redacted_domains_analysis,omitempty"`
	Errors                  []ErrorInfo              `json:"errors,omitempty"`
	Warnings                []ErrorInfo              `json:"warnings,omitempty"`
	ToolUsage               []ToolUsageInfo          `json:"tool_usage,omitempty"`
	MCPToolUsage            *MCPToolUsageData        `json:"mcp_tool_usage,omitempty"`
	CreatedItems            []CreatedItemReport      `json:"created_items,omitempty"`
}

// Finding represents a key insight discovered during audit
type Finding struct {
	Category    string `json:"category"`         // e.g., "error", "performance", "cost", "tooling"
	Severity    string `json:"severity"`         // "critical", "high", "medium", "low", "info"
	Title       string `json:"title"`            // Brief title
	Description string `json:"description"`      // Detailed description
	Impact      string `json:"impact,omitempty"` // What impact this has
}

// Recommendation represents an actionable suggestion
type Recommendation struct {
	Priority string `json:"priority"`          // "high", "medium", "low"
	Action   string `json:"action"`            // What to do
	Reason   string `json:"reason"`            // Why to do it
	Example  string `json:"example,omitempty"` // Example of how to implement
}

// PerformanceMetrics provides aggregated performance statistics
type PerformanceMetrics struct {
	TokensPerMinute float64 `json:"tokens_per_minute,omitempty"`
	CostEfficiency  string  `json:"cost_efficiency,omitempty"` // e.g., "good", "poor"
	AvgToolDuration string  `json:"avg_tool_duration,omitempty"`
	MostUsedTool    string  `json:"most_used_tool,omitempty"`
	NetworkRequests int     `json:"network_requests,omitempty"`
}

// OverviewData contains basic information about the workflow run
type OverviewData struct {
	RunID        int64      `json:"run_id" console:"header:Run ID"`
	WorkflowName string     `json:"workflow_name" console:"header:Workflow"`
	Status       string     `json:"status" console:"header:Status"`
	Conclusion   string     `json:"conclusion,omitempty" console:"header:Conclusion,omitempty"`
	CreatedAt    time.Time  `json:"created_at" console:"header:Created At"`
	StartedAt    time.Time  `json:"started_at,omitzero" console:"header:Started At,omitempty"`
	UpdatedAt    time.Time  `json:"updated_at,omitzero" console:"header:Updated At,omitempty"`

37	func buildAuditObservabilityInsights(processedRun ProcessedRun, metrics MetricsData, toolUsage []ToolUsageInfo, createdItems []CreatedItemReport) []ObservabilityInsight {
38		observabilityInsightsLog.Printf("Building audit observability insights: run_id=%d turns=%d tool_types=%d", processedRun.Run.DatabaseID, metrics.Turns, len(toolUsage))
39		insights := make([]ObservabilityInsight, 0, 5)
40		toolTypes := len(toolUsage)
41	
42		switch {
43		case metrics.Turns >= 12 || toolTypes >= 6:
44			insights = append(insights, ObservabilityInsight{
45				Category: "execution",
46				Severity: "medium",
47				Title:    "Exploratory execution path",
48				Summary:  fmt.Sprintf("The agent used %d turns across %d tool types, which indicates adaptive planning instead of a strictly linear path.", metrics.Turns, toolTypes),
49				Evidence: fmt.Sprintf("turns=%d tool_types=%d", metrics.Turns, toolTypes),
50			})
51		case metrics.Turns >= 6 || toolTypes >= 4:
52			insights = append(insights, ObservabilityInsight{
53				Category: "execution",
54				Severity: "info",
55				Title:    "Adaptive execution path",
56				Summary:  fmt.Sprintf("The run stayed moderately dynamic with %d turns and %d tool types.", metrics.Turns, toolTypes),
57				Evidence: fmt.Sprintf("turns=%d tool_types=%d", metrics.Turns, toolTypes),
58			})
59		default:
60			insights = append(insights, ObservabilityInsight{
61				Category: "execution",
62				Severity: "info",
63				Title:    "Directed execution path",
64				Summary:  fmt.Sprintf("The run remained relatively linear with %d turns and %d tool types.", metrics.Turns, toolTypes),
65				Evidence: fmt.Sprintf("turns=%d tool_types=%d", metrics.Turns, toolTypes),
66			})
67		}
68	
69		createdCount := len(createdItems)
70		safeItemsCount := processedRun.Run.SafeItemsCount
71		if createdCount > 0 || safeItemsCount > 0 {
72			insights = append(insights, ObservabilityInsight{
73				Category: "actuation",
74				Severity: "info",
75				Title:    "Write path executed",
76				Summary:  fmt.Sprintf("The workflow crossed from analysis into action, producing %d created item(s) and %d safe output action(s).", createdCount, safeItemsCount),
77				Evidence: fmt.Sprintf("created_items=%d safe_items=%d", createdCount, safeItemsCount),
78			})
79		} else {
80			insights = append(insights, ObservabilityInsight{
81				Category: "actuation",
82				Severity: "info",
83				Title:    "Read-only posture observed",
84				Summary:  "The workflow stayed in an analysis posture and did not emit any GitHub write actions.",
85				Evidence: "created_items=0 safe_items=0",
86			})
87		}
88	
89		frictionEvents := len(processedRun.MissingTools) + len(processedRun.MCPFailures) + len(processedRun.MissingData)
90		if frictionEvents > 0 {
91			severity := "medium"
92			if len(processedRun.MCPFailures) > 0 || frictionEvents >= 3 {

29	var copilotEventsJSONLLog = logger.New("cli:copilot_events_jsonl")
30	
31	// copilotEventsJSONLEntry represents a single event in a Copilot events.jsonl file.
32	// All events share the same envelope: type, id, timestamp, and a type-specific data object.
33	//
34	// The events.jsonl file is written by the Copilot CLI to:
35	//
36	//	~/.copilot/session-state/<session-uuid>/events.jsonl
37	//
38	// After artifact upload/download it is located at:
39	//
40	//	<logDir>/sandbox/agent/logs/copilot-session-state/<uuid>/events.jsonl
41	type copilotEventsJSONLEntry struct {
42		Type      string                      `json:"type"`
43		ID        string                      `json:"id"`
44		Timestamp string                      `json:"timestamp"`
45		ParentID  string                      `json:"parentId,omitempty"`
46		Data      copilotEventsJSONLEntryData `json:"data"`
47	}
48	
49	// copilotEventsJSONLEntryData holds the type-specific payload for each event.
50	// Fields are populated only for the relevant event types.
51	type copilotEventsJSONLEntryData struct {
52		// session.start fields
53		SessionID      string `json:"sessionId,omitempty"`
54		CopilotVersion string `json:"copilotVersion,omitempty"`
55	
56		// session.model_change fields
57		NewModel string `json:"newModel,omitempty"`
58	
59		// tool.execution_start fields
60		ToolCallID    string `json:"toolCallId,omitempty"`
61		ToolName      string `json:"toolName,omitempty"`
62		MCPServerName string `json:"mcpServerName,omitempty"`
63		MCPToolName   string `json:"mcpToolName,omitempty"`
64	
65		// tool.execution_complete fields
66		Success bool   `json:"success"`
67		Model   string `json:"model,omitempty"`
68	
69		// session.shutdown fields
70		ShutdownType         string                          `json:"shutdownType,omitempty"`
71		TotalPremiumRequests int                             `json:"totalPremiumRequests,omitempty"`
72		ModelMetrics         map[string]*copilotModelMetrics `json:"modelMetrics,omitempty"`
73	}
74	
75	// copilotModelMetrics holds per-model usage statistics from the session.shutdown event.
76	type copilotModelMetrics struct {
77		Requests *copilotRequestMetrics `json:"requests,omitempty"`
78		Usage    *copilotUsageMetrics   `json:"usage,omitempty"`
79	}
80	
81	// copilotRequestMetrics holds request count and cost for a model.
82	type copilotRequestMetrics struct {
83		Count int `json:"count"`
84		Cost  int `json:"cost"`
85	}
86	
87	// copilotUsageMetrics holds token usage for a model.
88	type copilotUsageMetrics struct {
89		InputTokens      int `json:"inputTokens"`
90		OutputTokens     int `json:"outputTokens"`
91		CacheReadTokens  int `json:"cacheReadTokens"`
92		CacheWriteTokens int `json:"cacheWriteTokens"`
93	}
94	
95	// findEventsJSONLFile searches for an events.jsonl file in logDir.
96	// It first checks the canonical location at
97	// sandbox/agent/logs/copilot-session-state/<uuid>/events.jsonl
98	// and then falls back to a full recursive walk of logDir.
99	// Returns the first path found, or an empty string if not found.
100	func findEventsJSONLFile(logDir string) string {
101		copilotEventsJSONLLog.Printf("Searching for events.jsonl in: %s", logDir)
102	
103		// Try the canonical location first (avoids a full directory walk in the common case)
104		sessionStateDir := filepath.Join(logDir, "sandbox", "agent", "logs", "copilot-session-state")
105		if canonicalPath := findFileInDir(sessionStateDir, "events.jsonl"); canonicalPath != "" {
106			copilotEventsJSONLLog.Printf("Found events.jsonl at canonical location: %s", canonicalPath)
107			return canonicalPath
108		}
109	
110		// Fall back to a recursive search of the full log directory
111		var foundPath string
112		_ = filepath.Walk(logDir, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {
113			if err != nil || info == nil {
114				return nil
115			}
116			if !info.IsDir() && info.Name() == "events.jsonl" && foundPath == "" {
117				foundPath = path
118				return errors.New("stop") // sentinel to stop walking
119			}
120			return nil
121		})
122	
123		if foundPath != "" {
124			copilotEventsJSONLLog.Printf("Found events.jsonl via recursive search: %s", foundPath)
125		} else {
126			copilotEventsJSONLLog.Printf("events.jsonl not found in: %s", logDir)
127		}
128		return foundPath
129	}
130	
131	// findFileInDir searches for a file by name within dir (recursively).
132	// Returns the first matching path, or empty string if not found.
133	func findFileInDir(dir, name string) string {
134		var found string
135		_ = filepath.Walk(dir, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {
136			if err != nil || info == nil {
137				return nil
138			}
139			if !info.IsDir() && info.Name() == name && found == "" {
140				found = path
141				return errors.New("stop")
142			}
143			return nil
144		})
145		return found
146	}
147	
148	// parseEventsJSONLFile parses a Copilot events.jsonl file and extracts log metrics.
149	//
150	// events.jsonl provides precise, structured data about a Copilot CLI session:
151	//   - "session.start":          session metadata (sessionId, copilotVersion)
152	//   - "user.message":           one per conversation turn (used to count turns)
153	//   - "tool.execution_start":   a tool invocation (data.toolName)
154	//   - "tool.execution_complete": completion of a tool call
155	//   - "session.shutdown":       session summary (totalPremiumRequests, modelMetrics)
156	//
157	// Returns the extracted metrics and nil on success, or empty metrics and an
158	// error if the file cannot be read or contains no recognizable events.
159	func parseEventsJSONLFile(path string, verbose bool) (workflow.LogMetrics, error) {
160		copilotEventsJSONLLog.Printf("Parsing events.jsonl from: %s", path)
161	
162		var metrics workflow.LogMetrics
163	
164		// Sanitize path to prevent traversal
165		cleanPath := filepath.Clean(path)
166	
167		file, err := os.Open(cleanPath)
168		if err != nil {
169			return metrics, fmt.Errorf("failed to open events.jsonl: %w", err)
170		}
171		defer file.Close()
172	
173		toolCallMap := make(map[string]*workflow.ToolCallInfo)
174		var currentSequence []string
175		turns := 0
176		totalTokens := 0
177		foundAnyEvent := false
178	
179		// Per-turn timestamps used to compute Time Between Turns (TBT)
180		var turnTimestamps []time.Time
181	
182		scanner := bufio.NewScanner(file)
183		buf := make([]byte, maxScannerBufferSize)
184		scanner.Buffer(buf, maxScannerBufferSize)
185	
186		for scanner.Scan() {
187			line := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
188			if line == "" || !strings.HasPrefix(line, "{") {
189				continue
190			}
191	
192			var entry copilotEventsJSONLEntry
193			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry); err != nil {
194				copilotEventsJSONLLog.Printf("Skipping malformed events.jsonl line: %v", err)
195				continue
196			}
197	
198			foundAnyEvent = true
199	
200			switch entry.Type {
201			case "session.start":
202				copilotEventsJSONLLog.Printf("session.start: sessionId=%s copilotVersion=%s",
203					entry.Data.SessionID, entry.Data.CopilotVersion)
204	
205			case "user.message":
206				// Each user message represents one conversation turn.
207				// Save the current tool sequence before starting a new turn.
208				turns++
209				if len(currentSequence) > 0 {
210					metrics.ToolSequences = append(metrics.ToolSequences, currentSequence)
211					currentSequence = []string{}
212				}
213				// Record the timestamp for TBT computation.
214				if entry.Timestamp != "" {
215					if ts, parseErr := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, entry.Timestamp); parseErr == nil {
216						turnTimestamps = append(turnTimestamps, ts)
217					} else if ts, parseErr = time.Parse(time.RFC3339, entry.Timestamp); parseErr == nil {
218						turnTimestamps = append(turnTimestamps, ts)
219					}
220				}
221				copilotEventsJSONLLog.Printf("user.message: turn=%d", turns)
222	
223			case "tool.execution_start":
224				// Record the tool invocation and add to the current turn's sequence.
225				toolName := entry.Data.ToolName
226				if toolName != "" {
227					currentSequence = append(currentSequence, toolName)
228					if toolInfo, exists := toolCallMap[toolName]; exists {
229						toolInfo.CallCount++
230					} else {
231						toolCallMap[toolName] = &workflow.ToolCallInfo{
232							Name:      toolName,
233							CallCount: 1,
234						}
235					}
236					copilotEventsJSONLLog.Printf("tool.execution_start: %s", toolName)
237				}
238	
239			case "session.shutdown":
240				// Aggregate token usage across all models from modelMetrics.
241				// modelMetrics provides the most accurate token counts; fall back to
242				// totalPremiumRequests only when modelMetrics is absent.
243				for model, m := range entry.Data.ModelMetrics {
244					if m.Usage != nil {
245						modelTokens := m.Usage.InputTokens + m.Usage.OutputTokens
246						totalTokens += modelTokens
247						copilotEventsJSONLLog.Printf("session.shutdown: model=%s inputTokens=%d outputTokens=%d",
248							model, m.Usage.InputTokens, m.Usage.OutputTokens)
249					}
250				}
251				if totalTokens == 0 && entry.Data.TotalPremiumRequests > 0 {
252					// Fallback: use premium requests as a proxy when token counts are unavailable
253					totalTokens = entry.Data.TotalPremiumRequests
254					copilotEventsJSONLLog.Printf("session.shutdown: using totalPremiumRequests=%d as token proxy",
255						entry.Data.TotalPremiumRequests)
256				}
257				copilotEventsJSONLLog.Printf("session.shutdown: type=%s totalPremiumRequests=%d totalTokens=%d",
258					entry.Data.ShutdownType, entry.Data.TotalPremiumRequests, totalTokens)
259			}
260		}
261	
262		if scanErr := scanner.Err(); scanErr != nil {
263			return metrics, fmt.Errorf("error reading events.jsonl: %w", scanErr)
264		}
265	
266		if !foundAnyEvent {
267			return metrics, errors.New("no events found in events.jsonl")
268		}
269	
270		// Flush any remaining tool sequence
271		if len(currentSequence) > 0 {
272			metrics.ToolSequences = append(metrics.ToolSequences, currentSequence)
273		}
274	
275		// Convert tool call map to slice
276		for _, toolInfo := range toolCallMap {
277			metrics.ToolCalls = append(metrics.ToolCalls, *toolInfo)
278		}
279	
280		metrics.TokenUsage = totalTokens
281		metrics.Turns = turns
282	
283		// Compute Time Between Turns (TBT) from per-turn timestamps.
284		// TBT[i] = timestamp[i] - timestamp[i-1] for i > 0. Two or more timestamps
285		// are required to measure at least one interval. Only positive intervals are
286		// included so that identical or out-of-order timestamps don't skew the statistics.
287		if len(turnTimestamps) >= 2 {
288			var tbtStats stats.StatVar
289			for i := 1; i < len(turnTimestamps); i++ {
290				tbt := turnTimestamps[i].Sub(turnTimestamps[i-1])
291				if tbt > 0 {
292					tbtStats.Add(float64(tbt))
293				}
294			}
295			if tbtStats.Count() > 0 {
296				metrics.AvgTimeBetweenTurns = time.Duration(tbtStats.Mean())
297				metrics.MaxTimeBetweenTurns = time.Duration(tbtStats.Max())
298				metrics.MedianTimeBetweenTurns = time.Duration(tbtStats.Median())
299				metrics.StdDevTimeBetweenTurns = time.Duration(tbtStats.SampleStdDev())
300				copilotEventsJSONLLog.Printf("TBT computed: avg=%s max=%s median=%s stddev=%s intervals=%d",
301					metrics.AvgTimeBetweenTurns, metrics.MaxTimeBetweenTurns,
302					metrics.MedianTimeBetweenTurns, metrics.StdDevTimeBetweenTurns, tbtStats.Count())
303			}
304		}
305	
306		copilotEventsJSONLLog.Printf("Parsed events.jsonl: turns=%d totalTokens=%d toolCalls=%d sequences=%d",
307			turns, totalTokens, len(toolCallMap), len(metrics.ToolSequences))
308	
309		if verbose {
310			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, console.FormatSuccessMessage(
311				fmt.Sprintf("Parsed events.jsonl: %d turns, %d tokens, %d tool calls",
312					turns, totalTokens, len(toolCallMap))))
313		}
314	
315		return metrics, nil
316	}

383			// Supplement firewall analysis with blocked domains extracted directly from

340	// ValidateHeredocDelimiter checks that a delimiter is safe for use inside
341	// single-quoted heredoc syntax (<< 'DELIM'). Rejects delimiters containing
342	// single quotes, newlines, carriage returns, or non-printable characters
343	// that could break the generated shell/YAML.
344	func ValidateHeredocDelimiter(delimiter string) error {
345		for _, r := range delimiter {
346			switch {
347			case r == '\'':
348				return fmt.Errorf("heredoc delimiter %q contains single quote", delimiter)
349			case r == '\n', r == '\r':
350				return fmt.Errorf("heredoc delimiter %q contains newline", delimiter)
351			case r < 0x20 && r != '\t':
352				return fmt.Errorf("heredoc delimiter %q contains non-printable character %U", delimiter, r)
353			}
354		}
355		return nil
356	}

358	// PrettifyToolName removes "mcp__" prefix and formats tool names nicely
359	func PrettifyToolName(toolName string) string {
360		// Handle MCP tools: "mcp__github__search_issues" -> "github_search_issues"
361		// Avoid colons and leave underscores as-is
362		if strings.HasPrefix(toolName, "mcp__") {
363			parts := strings.Split(toolName, "__")
364			if len(parts) >= 3 {
365				provider := parts[1]
366				method := strings.Join(parts[2:], "_")
367				return fmt.Sprintf("%s_%s", provider, method)
368			}
369			// If format is unexpected, just remove the mcp__ prefix
370			return strings.TrimPrefix(toolName, "mcp__")
371		}
372	
373		// Handle bash specially - keep as "bash"
374		if strings.ToLower(toolName) == "bash" {
375			return "bash"
376		}
377	
378		// Return other tool names as-is
379		return toolName
380	}
